tools.name: get_dashboard
tools.description: Get the full dashboard overview showing all monitored API endpoints with their current status (up/degraded/down/maintenance), response time, uptime percentage, and health score.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: Get the overall system health check showing database status and server health.

tools.name: get_endpoint_status
tools.description: Get detailed status of a specific API endpoint including current verdict, response time, uptime history, recent pings, and 24h uptime stats.

id.description: The endpoint UUID (e.g. d66bbde2-d239-48ff-9064-25a24e171459 for API Gateway)

tools.name: get_endpoint_pings
tools.description: Get recent ping history for a specific endpoint showing response times and verdicts. Use this to investigate latency patterns or failure history.

id.description: The endpoint UUID to query ping history for

tools.name: get_public_status
tools.description: Get the public-facing status page data showing all endpoints with their status for sharing with users or embedding on external pages.

tools.name: pause_endpoint
tools.description: Pause monitoring for a specific endpoint. Paused endpoints are not pinged and do not generate alerts. Use this during planned maintenance.

id.description: The endpoint UUID to pause monitoring for

tools.name: test_endpoint
tools.description: Trigger an immediate test ping for a specific endpoint. Returns the live response time and status. Use this to verify an endpoint is reachable.

id.description: The endpoint UUID to test immediately

tools.name: diagnose_incident
tools.description: Run automated incident diagnostic on a specific endpoint. Classifies incident type (timeout/5xx/DNS/latency), analyzes patterns from recent pings, and provides specific recommendations.

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the internal runbook knowledge base for troubleshooting guides, postmortems, and migration guides related to API incidents.

query.description: Search query for finding relevant runbooks

tools.name: resolve_incident
tools.description: Generate a structured incident resolution plan for a diagnosed endpoint. After the AI calls diagnose_incident, call this to produce a prioritized action plan (P1/P2/P3) with concrete steps the user can take to fix the incident, plus safe actions (pause monitoring, test ping) the AI can offer to execute.

endpointId.description: The endpoint UUID that was just diagnosed

incidentType.description: The incident type from the diagnostic (server_error_5xx, dns_failure, timeout, latency_spike, client_error_4xx, certificate_expiry, unknown)

severity.description: Severity level: critical, high, medium, or low
